title: The Citadel Awakens
synopsis: What 800-53 is, why it exists, how to read a control, why baselines moved to 800-53B, and where it all fits in the RMF.

title: The Outer Walls
synopsis: Who gets in, and what they may touch: access control, identity, and physical security.

title: The Watchtowers
synopsis: See the intruder, keep the system honest, and respond: audit, integrity, incident response.

title: The Keepers of the Pact
synopsis: People and privacy: awareness training, personnel security, and PII processing & transparency.

title: The High Council
synopsis: Govern and assess: risk assessment, planning, program management, and assessment & authorization.

title: The Forge
synopsis: Build, configure, maintain, and source securely, including the supply chain.

title: The Vaults & Lifelines
synopsis: Protect the data and survive disaster: communications protection, media protection, contingency planning.

title: Siege Night
synopsis: Act I finale: defense in depth, the full RMF at a glance, and how 800-53 gets used in practice.

title: The Campaign Briefing
synopsis: One real system walked end to end through the RMF — categorize, select, tailor, implement, assess, authorize, monitor — with a named human cast (System Owner, AO, ISSO, Privacy Officer, Assessor).

title: The Campaign: the RMF
synopsis: Act II begins. The 7-step Risk Management Framework and how the NIST document family fits together.

title: Know Your Realm
synopsis: Security categorization: rating Confidentiality/Integrity/Availability, the high-water mark, minimum requirements.

title: The Armory: Baselines
synopsis: Control baselines (Low 149 / Moderate 287 / High 370 / Privacy 96) and how to tailor them with overlays.

title: The Reckoning
synopsis: Assessment methods (examine/interview/test), the SAR and POA&M, authorization (ATO), continuous monitoring.
